items.headline: U.S. Markets Close at Record Highs Amid Strong Jobs Data, Tech Rally
items.summary: U.S. stock markets soared to new record highs on Friday, May 9, 2026, driven by a stronger-than-expected jobs report for April and continued enthusiasm in the technology sector. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both reached fresh all-time highs, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average also posted gains. Market sentiment, described by StoneX as 'euphoria,' propelled equities higher, despite lingering geopolitical tensions in the Middle East causing oil prices to rise and a broader selloff in the commodity sector. However, underlying concerns include U.S. consumer confidence hitting an all-time low and mortgage rates reaching a four-week high. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Micron Soars on AI Demand
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) saw a significant jump, reflecting strong demand in the AI sector.

items.headline: Intel Gains Amidst AI Infrastructure Buildout
items.summary: Intel ($INTC) surged, benefiting from the ongoing AI infrastructure buildout.

items.headline: AMD Continues Upward Trend
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) posted strong daily gains, fueled by robust AI demand.

items.headline: Forward Air Experiences Sharp Decline
items.summary: Forward Air ($FWRD) was among the steepest decliners. (Data as of May 8, 2026)

items.headline: TSS, Inc. Sees Significant Drop
items.summary: TSS, Inc. ($TSSI) recorded a substantial loss. (Data as of May 8, 2026)

items.headline: Meta Platforms Down on Capex Guidance
items.summary: Meta Platforms ($META) shares fell after the company raised its 2026 capital expenditure guidance, despite beating earnings expectations.

items.headline: Technology and Energy Lead Sector Performance
items.summary: The Technology ($XLK) and Energy ($XLE) sectors are currently leading the market, with Technology up 6.7% for the week ending May 8, 2026. Conversely, the Energy sector experienced a weekly decline of 5.55%. Other sectors like Materials ($XLB), Utilities ($XLU), and Consumer Discretionary ($XLY) are showing improving momentum, while Healthcare ($XLV) and Financials ($XLF) remain lagging.

items.headline: Robust April Jobs Report Fuels Market Optimism
items.summary: The U.S. economy added 115,000 nonfarm payroll jobs in April, nearly double economists' expectations, with the unemployment rate holding steady at 4.3%. This strong jobs data was a key factor in the market's record-setting performance.

items.headline: Fed's Rate Path Debated Amid Fractured FOMC
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, but the April 29th FOMC meeting saw four dissents, indicating a significant division among policymakers regarding the future path of rates. Outgoing Chair Jerome Powell's final meeting highlighted this split, and incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, expected to be approved next week, will inherit a historically fractured committee. Some Fed officials are issuing strong warnings against early rate cuts due to persistent inflation risks.

items.headline: Big Tech Earnings Show AI-Driven Growth, Capex Concerns
items.summary: Major tech companies like Alphabet ($GOOGL), Microsoft ($MSFT), Amazon ($AMZN), and Meta Platforms ($META) have recently reported earnings. Meta showed strong revenue growth driven by AI, but its stock declined after raising 2026 capital expenditure guidance. Alphabet saw a 63% spike in Google Cloud revenue, while Amazon Web Services (AWS) revenue climbed 28%, its fastest growth in nearly four years. Microsoft's Azure also delivered 40% revenue growth, all underscoring the significant impact of AI demand on cloud computing. Overall, Q1 earnings season has exceeded expectations, with a high percentage of S&P 500 companies beating EPS estimates.

items.headline: China's Foreign Trade Maintains Solid Growth in April
items.summary: China's foreign trade in yuan-denominated terms grew 14.2% year-on-year in April, with exports rising 9.8% and imports increasing by 20.6%. This data, released on Saturday, May 9, 2026, highlights the resilience and vitality of China's imports and exports.
